title: First-party C++ authoring engine
summary: cpp_authoring.lua is the sole interactive owner for structured templates, the dollar type language, live suggestions, include edits, and placeholder navigation.
details: cpp_type_snippets.lua is now a grammar/formatting library: it resolves type expressions, provides candidates and previews, class context, protected-context classification, and standard-header insertion.
cpp_authoring.lua owns FileType mappings, Space and Tab dispatch, the structured catalog, preview extmarks, commands, and integration with vim.snippet.
Tree-sitter protects comments, ordinary/raw strings, and character literals; the old one-line quote scanner remains only as a parser-unavailable fallback and pure-test seam.
Catalog triggers include class/rule-of-N templates, index/range loops, println helpers, a vector initializer, a CUDA launch, and a block comment; dsk_* aliases remain compatible.
Dollar expressions and structured templates share one end-of-line suggestion surface. Tab expands an exact expression or completes a unique head; Space commits an exact expression.
Unknown expressions are left in the buffer, and Enter no longer accepts an unselected cmp candidate.
rationale: One interaction owner makes session cleanup, suggestion gating, context rules, undo behavior, and future structured actions testable together.
The grammar remains independently pure enough for exhaustive string tests while the orchestration has real mode/mapping coverage.

evidence.claim: Real Space/Tab mappings, mirror updates, legacy aliases, raw-string/comment gates, and typo preservation are exercised.
The compositional grammar and include/format behavior remain exhaustively pinned.

questions: Where should a new C++ authoring template be added?
How are dollar suggestions and structured snippets coordinated?
Which tests reproduce snippet interaction failures?

last_verified.method: Implementation boundary review and focused interaction suite.
